channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Is your phone listening to everything you say? It’s complicated
item.description: Google, Instagram, Facebook and weather apps can track your location and use that data to serve you ads; here's what you can do to protect your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wikipedia Under Threat: A Breaking Point for the Online Library
item.description: Republicans ramp up attacks against Wikipedia, exposing specific editors and threatening its existence. The online library's cofounders and executives are worried about the rising noise of criticism from Elon Musk and others.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Lenovo ThinkBook Flip AI PC: A Unique Business Laptop with a Foldable OLED Screen
item.description: Lenovo is set to unveil a business laptop with a foldable OLED screen, effectively doubling the available screen space when unfolded. The ThinkBook Flip AI PC also includes a built-in keyboard and shares similarities with other dual-screen laptops.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Amazon just overtook Walmart in revenue for the first time
item.description: Amazon overtakes Walmart in terms of quarterly revenue, but how long will it last before it regains pole position?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Swedish Authorities Investigate Damaged Baltic Sea Cable - 2024-02-16 14:45:00
item.description: Swedish authorities investigate damaged Baltic Sea cable, sparking fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Substack bets big on video as TikTok’s future remains uncertain
item.description: With TikTok’s future in the U.S. still uncertain, Substack is doubling down on attracting video creators. As of yesterday, creators can now publish video posts directly from the Substack app—a feature previously limited to desktop.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Rivian records first gross profit as EV policy risks loom
item.description: The California-based company's fourth quarter result easily topped the $32 million expected by Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: HP is apparently forcing customer support callers to wait 15 minutes before talking to anyone
item.description: HP wants customers to self-service their problems so much it’s reportedly adding a 15-minute wait to calls.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The iPhone 17 could launch with a display upgrade that makes the iPhone 17 Pro seem questionable
item.description: A new rumor suggests the entire iPhone 17 family could launch with 120Hz displays, finally bringing Apple up to par with the competition years after high-refresh rate screens became standard for smartphones.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Better student housing with a unified data platform
item.description: At the end of 2023, Chicago-based Article Student Living was acquired by a global real estate investment company, which allowed the business to expand, and enabled it to make key investments in the high-demand student housing market.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Huawei Band 10 is here, and it's packing a secret mood-tracking weapon
item.description: Looking for a budget fitness tracker? The Huawei Band 10 might be worth a look. It comes in a variety of new colors and has some surprising features.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: INE Security’s Cybersecurity and IT Training Enhances Career Stability in Tech
item.description: 2025 marks a time of unprecedented volatility in the technology job market. On one hand, dependence on technology is soaring. The growth of AI and machine learning is propelling a surge in new technologies, tactics, and ideas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Deno update brings OpenTelemetry integration
item.description: Deno Land has released Deno 2.2, a new version of the Deno runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript that features built-in OpenTelemetry for monitoring logs, metrics, and traces. The new Deno release also features Linter updates that include a new plugin system and new rules for React and Preact users.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AT&T debuts SplitPay, making it easier to split the bill on shared plans
item.description: The upside of friends, roommates, or family members sharing a multiline phone plan is everyone has a lower monthly bill. The downside, however, is one person is ultimately responsible for both paying that bill—and gathering what is owed to him or her from their friends each month.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Everything we know about Apple’s C1 5G modem in iPhone 16e
item.description: There’s a lot to like about Apple’s newest $599 iPhone 16e, but one of the more interesting features in the device is the Apple-designed 5G modem. Apple has spent at least seven years developing the chip, but gave it just a few short seconds of time when it announced the smartphone this week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition
item.description: Learn how OSINT is shaping the future of intelligence and security. The post OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO
item.description: Katie Arrington's return to DoD comes as her lawyers work out a settlement with the government related to the suspension of her security clearance in 2021. The post Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Humanoid robots bust dance moves alongside humans
item.description: Unitree H1 robots dance at gala, blending AI and tradition. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson examines the convergence of cutting-edge technology and traditional culture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 22 new tricks for your Google Pixel Clock app
item.description: Google’s Pixel phones are practically overflowing with useful stuff. And some of the best options of all are things you’ve probably never even noticed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dell secures deal worth more than $5B in Elon Musk's xAI startup company
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI is one step closer to becoming a reality after Dell secured a deal worth more than $5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, NewsNation

item.title: How to keep AI hallucinations out of your code
item.description: It turns out androids do dream, and their dreams are often strange. In the early days of generative AI, we got human hands with eight fingers and recipes for making pizza sauce from glue. Now, developers working with AI-assisted coding tools are also finding AI hallucinations in their code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5
